steps.title: Support Morrison's editorial aim
steps.content: The scholar claims that Morrison likely aimed to strengthen Black writers' presence among Random House authors. Strong support would show that Black authors became more represented during her time as an editor.

steps.title: Reject indirect evidence
steps.content: Choice B concerns Morrison's influence as a novelist. Choice C concerns sales and acclaim after her editing career. Choice D concerns stylistic features of works she edited. A rise in the percentage of Black authors during the early 1970s directly supports the claim. The answer is A.
